quote: We have shown that the majority of these putative proteins from the Sakai strain are translocated by the LEE-encoded T3SS (Table 1).
note: NleB-family effector encoded in prophage Sp4; gene sits in prophage but is translocated by the LEE injectisome per Tobe 2006.

quote: We have shown that the majority of these putative proteins from the Sakai strain are translocated by the LEE-encoded T3SS (Table 1).
note: NleB-family effector in prophage Sp9; translocated by LEE T3SS, not the prophage.

quote: We have shown that the majority of these putative proteins from the Sakai strain are translocated by the LEE-encoded T3SS (Table 1).
note: NleG family is the largest LEE-T3SS effector family in Sakai (14 members); majority confirmed LEE-translocated. Gene sits in prophage Sp9.

quote: We have shown that the majority of these putative proteins from the Sakai strain are translocated by the LEE-encoded T3SS (Table 1).
note: NleG2-2 carries Table 1 translocation-assay code (SCF) in Tobe 2006; LEE-translocated effector encoded in prophage Sp11.

note: NleG family LEE-T3SS effector; gene in prophage Sp13.

quote: We have shown that the majority of these putative proteins from the Sakai strain are translocated by the LEE-encoded T3SS (Table 1).
note: NleG family LEE-T3SS effector; gene in prophage Sp9. (Note: an NleG9' paralog is an H-only pseudogene in Tobe Table 1; this NleG9 entry resolved at family level.)

In Tobe 2006 Table 1, EspL1 carries the H code (homologous to known effector but not confirmed by translocation assay). Only EspL2 was confirmed LEE-translocated. No experimental tie to the LEE injectisome; stays type-level positive.
EspL3' is H-only (homology, pseudogene) in Tobe 2006 Table 1; not translocation-confirmed. Only EspL2 was confirmed.
EspL4 is H-only (homology, not confirmed by translocation) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Only EspL2 was confirmed LEE-translocated.

quote: We found that EspN, which is type-III-secreted in a sepL mutant, showed sequence similarity to part of cytotoxic necrotizing factor 1 from E. coli.
note: EspN is type-III-secreted in a sepL (LEE-encoded) mutant per Tobe 2006, tying it to the LEE T3SS secretion machinery. Gene in prophage Sp9.
All EspR members (EspR1-4) carry the H code (homology-only, not confirmed by translocation) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. No experimental tie to the LEE injectisome.
EspR3 is H-only (homology, not translocation-confirmed) in Tobe 2006 Table 1; gene in prophage CP-933U. No experimental tie to a specific T3SS instance.
EspR4 is H-only (homology, not translocation-confirmed) in Tobe 2006 Table 1; gene in prophage CP-933U.
EspV' in Sakai is a predicted pseudogene that scored negative in the beta-lactamase translocation assay (Neg + psi codes) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Not a confirmed LEE effector in this strain.

quote: EspW, which we have shown to be translocated, shares 29% identity with the C-terminal domain of HopPmaA/HopW...
note: EspW confirmed translocated (Table 1 code SC) by the LEE T3SS in Tobe 2006; gene in phage-like element SpLE3.
EspX1 is H-only (homology, not translocation-confirmed) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Of EspX1-7, only EspX2 (translocation) and EspX7 (secretome) were positive.

quote: Likewise, of the seven SopA/PipB homologs (EspX1-7) identified in the genome, two (EspX2 and EspX7) were found to be secreted or translocated (Table 1).
note: EspX2 carries the B (beta-lactamase translocation) code in Tobe 2006 Table 1, confirming LEE-T3SS translocation. Gene in prophage Sp4.
EspX3' is H-only (homology, pseudogene) in Tobe 2006 Table 1; not translocation-confirmed. Only EspX2/EspX7 were positive among EspX1-7.
EspX4 scored negative (Neg code) in the translocation assay in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Not a confirmed LEE effector.
EspX5 is H-only (homology, not translocation-confirmed) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Only EspX2/EspX7 were positive among EspX1-7.
EspX6 is H-only (homology, not translocation-confirmed) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Only EspX2/EspX7 were positive among EspX1-7.

quote: Likewise, of the seven SopA/PipB homologs (EspX1-7) identified in the genome, two (EspX2 and EspX7) were found to be secreted or translocated (Table 1).
note: EspX7 found in the secreted proteome (S code) of the LEE-dependent sepL secretome in Tobe 2006 Table 1, tying it to the LEE T3SS. Gene in prophage Sp9.

quote: Two of these proteins proved positive in translocation assays, although none was detected in the survey of the secreted proteome (Table 1).
note: EspY1 is one of the two EspY proteins positive in the LEE-T3SS translocation assay (Table 1 code B) in Tobe 2006.
EspY2 is H-only (homology, not translocation-confirmed) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Only EspY1 and EspY4 were positive among EspY1-5; EspY family absent from secretome.
EspY3 is H-only (homology, not translocation-confirmed) in Tobe 2006 Table 1. Only EspY1 and EspY4 were positive among EspY1-5.

quote: Two of these proteins proved positive in translocation assays, although none was detected in the survey of the secreted proteome (Table 1).
note: EspY4 is one of the two EspY proteins positive in the LEE-T3SS translocation assay (Table 1 code B) in Tobe 2006.
EspY5' is H-only (homology, pseudogene) in Tobe 2006 Table 1; not translocation-confirmed. Only EspY1 and EspY4 were positive among EspY1-5.

quote: they were only secreted by the WT and the sepD mutant strains, not by the escN mutant, confirming that their secretion is dependent on the LEE-encoded T3SS.
note: NleG-family effectors in C. rodentium are secreted in an escN-dependent (LEE-encoded T3SS ATPase) manner (Deng 2010 secretome). NleG family among non-LEE effectors confirmed LEE-dependent. (Locus-tag mapping flag: NCBI annotates ROD_31501 as a putative exported protein; true NleG tags in ICC168 are ROD_16511/40971/48891 - verify upstream mapping.)

quote: NleG1Cr, NleG7Cr, and NleG8Cr were detected only in the secreted fraction for the wild-type strain, confirming their T3SS-dependent secretion.
note: NleG1 in C. rodentium is T3SS-dependently secreted/translocated by the LEE injectisome (Popov 2022). (Locus-tag flag: NCBI annotates ROD_21621 as hisA; true nleG1 tag in ICC168 is ROD_16511 - verify upstream mapping.)

quote: NleG1Cr, NleG7Cr, and NleG8Cr were detected only in the secreted fraction for the wild-type strain, confirming their T3SS-dependent secretion.
note: NleG7 in C. rodentium is LEE-T3SS-dependently secreted (Popov 2022). (Locus-tag flag: NCBI annotates ROD_25881 as a hypothetical prophage protein; true nleG7 tag in ICC168 is ROD_48891 - verify upstream mapping.)

quote: NleG1Cr, NleG7Cr, and NleG8Cr were detected only in the secreted fraction for the wild-type strain, confirming their T3SS-dependent secretion.
note: NleG8 in C. rodentium is LEE-T3SS-dependently secreted (Popov 2022). (Locus-tag flag: NCBI annotates ROD_15971 as a hypothetical protein/DUF2554; true nleG8 tag in ICC168 is ROD_40971 - verify upstream mapping.)

No literature support. The Frontiers 2013 review (primary_ref) does not mention VPA1374 or VopX; its T3SS2 effector list is VopC/T/Z/A/V/L. UniProt Q87GE2 annotates VPA1374 as 'Uncharacterized protein' with no Vop synonym or effector annotation. 'VopX' in the literature is a Vibrio cholerae effector, not V. parahaemolyticus. Stays type-level positive; possible locus-tag/name mislabel upstream.

quote: We conclude that, although LoxA activity predominantly localizes to the periplasm, a significant fraction of the active protein is also secreted extracellularly by an Xcp-dependent process.
note: LoxA (PA1169) is secreted by the Xcp T2SS (direct delta-xcp evidence, Vance et al. 2004 PNAS). Corroborated by Bleves/Filloux 2011 review (10.3389/fmicb.2011.00155) listing LoxA under Xcp. T2SS_04 = Xcp.

quote: In contrast, there is only one Hxc-dependent substrate, namely LapA, known as low-molecular weight alkaline phosphatase (Ball et al., 2002).
note: Bleves/Filloux 2011 review places PmpA (PA0572) in the Xcp T2SS column (Table 1, citing Bleves et al. 2010), and states Hxc's only substrate is LapA, excluding PmpA from Hxc. Resolved to Xcp (T2SS_04).

Could not obtain literature support. The bioRxiv primary_ref blocks automated fetching and its abstract does not name EC042_4675/Tle3/Sci-2. EC042_4675 returns no hits in NCBI Gene/Protein/UniProt and sits ~100 genes downstream of both E. coli 042 T6SS clusters (sci-1: EC042_4524-4555; sci-2/AggR: EC042_4562-4577), not within sci-2. The documented EAEC 042 Tle phospholipase is Tle1, a Sci-1 effector (Flaugnatti 2016/2020). Stays type-level positive pending preprint full-text verification.
